title: Chimes
content:
Brief, on a flying night,
    From the shaken tower,
    A flock of bells take flight,
    And go with the hour.

    Like birds from the cote to the gales,
    Abrupt--O hark!
    A fleet of bells set sails,
    And go to the dark.

    Sudden the cold airs swing.
    Alone, aloud,
    A verse of bells takes wing
    And flies with the cloud.
meaning:
Alice Christiana Thompson Meynell's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Chimes"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
